title: Xylopleurum multicaule
intro: Xylopleurum multicaule, commonly known as the 'Mountain Xylopleurum', is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to Australia. It has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Xylopleurum multicaule include flavonoids, limonoids, and coumarins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are among the most common herbal preparations made with Xylopleurum multicaule.
taxonomy: Xylopleurum multicaule is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, which includes citrus and rue plants. This plant is native to Australia and is known for its clusters of small, white flowers. The genus Xylopleurum is part of the larger Rutaceae family, which is native to the Southern Hemisphere. Xylopleurum multicaule is often found in dry and open woodland areas, and its classification as part of the Rutaceae family reflects its close relationship with other plants in this family. The classification of Xylopleurum multicaule is also supported by its characteristics, such as its fragrant leaves and clusters of small flowers.
